summary: Iteration 38 — Expected Koli Summary feature: backend + frontend (all 5 panels) verified. 8/8 pytest backend tests passed. All 5 frontend panels (Management/Plan/Warehouse/Dashboard/Operator) render correct data-testid'd ExpectedKoliSummary cards with matching numbers from backend (283 koli, 7 jobs, %9.6 completion). Operator panel correctly filters to only the selected machine (40x40 ICM → 120 koli, 2 jobs, %20 completion). Dashboard /live endpoint exposes summary.expected_summary with identical shape.
critical_code_review_comments: /api/jobs/expected-summary calculation logic is identical to dashboard._build_expected_summary (DRY duplication ~30 LOC). Consider extracting a shared helper in a util module to avoid drift between the two endpoints.

Active job pool query {status: {$in: [pending, in_progress, paused]}} is used in 3+ places (jobs.py expected-summary, dashboard.py 3 separate find calls). A single aggregation pipeline could replace 3 round-trips in dashboard/live for a perf win when job count grows.

computeExpectedSummary helper in ExpectedKoliSummary.js mirrors backend math verbatim — good fallback when /api/jobs/expected-summary is unavailable, but ensure both formulas stay in sync (no rounding diff: backend uses round(_,1); JS uses Math.round(_*1000)/10 — equivalent).

Operator panel relies on client-side computeExpectedSummary(jobs, machineId) rather than /api/jobs/expected-summary?machine_id=… — works correctly but doubles maintenance surface. Suggest standardizing to server endpoint.
Response shape is intentionally flat (no pagination) — by_machine could grow unbounded if many machines added. Current 8-machine setup is fine.
test_credentials: management=buse11993, plan=emrecan/testtest12, warehouse=depo1/depo123, dashboard=buse4, operator=ali/134679 (machine: 40x40 ICM)
seed_data_creation: None — read-only tests against existing 7 active jobs (283 koli remaining).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 38 expected-koli summary feature fully verified end-to-end. Backend endpoint GET /api/jobs/expected-summary (+ optional machine_id filter) and dashboard.summary.expected_summary both pass 8/8 pytest. All 5 frontend panels render correct data-testid={management|plan|warehouse|dashboard|operator}-expected-koli cards with matching numbers. Operator panel uses client-side computeExpectedSummary(jobs, machineId) — correctly machine-scoped. Flow-test (partial shift report → remaining decrease) was NOT executed in this iteration because it requires destructive state mutation; if needed in future, use shift_end_report POST against an active in_progress job and re-check summary.
